concept_name: Magnetic Field of a Long Solenoid (B = μ₀nI inside, ≈0 outside)
source_book: DC Pandey Vol 2 Ch.26 §26.8 (Magnetic field due to a long solenoid) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Diamond #4 of the magnetism chapter (M4 binary-gate validator). The atomic concept being taught is the *direction* (axial inside, near-zero outside; right-hand rule grip swap from wire-RHR) and *magnitude* (B = μ₀nI, with n = turns per unit length). PRIMARY aha lives in STATE_4: each current element's radial field is annihilated by its diametric twin across the ring (STATE_3) so only the axial components survive and ADD into one uniform axial field (STATE_4) — what looks like chaotic overlapping circles becomes one uniform axial field. SUPPORTING aha in STATE_6: the right-hand grip swaps roles — for a straight wire thumb=I, fingers=B, but for a solenoid fingers wrap WITH the current loops and thumb gives B inside. Four most common misconceptions targeted: B is radial (looks like spokes), outside is just a weaker version of inside, total turns N matters (not density n), and the wire-RHR transfers unchanged to solenoids. Conceptual-only ship per MAGNETISM_ARCHITECTURE.md M1–M6 carve-out; board mode (M7) and competitive mode (M8) deferred. Diamond #4 is the binary-gate validator for the recursive-bootstrap method: templated from Diamond #1 magnetic_field_wire, must ship with ≤1 round of human iteration to prove the architect→physics_author→json_author→quality_auditor pipeline.

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture the MRI machine in a Bangalore hospital — the tube the patient slides into. That tube is a giant solenoid, kilometres of copper wire coiled tightly around the patient's bore. When current flows through those coils, the inside of the tube fills with a powerful, uniform magnetic field — 1.5 tesla, ten thousand times stronger than Earth's field. The patient's body sits in that uniform field, and hydrogen atoms inside their tissues respond. The whole imaging trick depends on one piece of physics: the field inside a long solenoid is UNIFORM and AXIAL. If the field varied from point to point inside the tube, the image would be unreadable. The MRI works because B = μ₀nI is constant everywhere inside.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture the starter motor in a Mumbai auto-rickshaw. When the driver turns the key, current surges through a small solenoid coil; the resulting axial field yanks an iron core inward, which mechanically pushes the starter pinion into the engine flywheel. No solenoid → no engine start. Every auto-rickshaw on Indian roads carries this concept under the hood.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture a doorbell button in a Chennai apartment. Press it and a small solenoid coil inside the bell housing energizes; the resulting axial field pulls an iron plunger that strikes the chime. Release the button, the field collapses, a spring snaps the plunger back. Solenoids turn an electrical pulse into a mechanical kick — the same principle as the MRI's giant uniform field, scaled down to a single chime strike.

N_demo.name: Total turn count (display only — for STATE_7 stretch demonstrative)

L_demo.name: Solenoid length (display only — paired with N_demo to show n = N/L)

formulas.n_definition: n = N / L (turns per unit length, units /m)
formulas.axial_superposition: B_axial_total = sum of B_axial from each turn — radial components cancel between adjacent turns; only axial components survive
formulas.B_outside_long_limit: B_outside ≈ 0 (contributions from turns at different axial positions cancel almost everywhere outside)
formulas.rhr_solenoid: Right-hand grip — fingers curl WITH the current loops, thumb gives B inside (role-swap from wire-RHR where thumb is current)
formulas.reverse_current: Reversing current direction reverses B_inside direction; magnitude |B| = μ₀·n·|I| is unchanged
formulas.stretch_demonstrative: If N stays fixed and L doubles, n halves, B halves

constraints: Long-solenoid idealization: L >> R (length much greater than radius of one turn). Formula B = μ₀·n·I is exact only in this limit.
End effects ignored: at the open ends, B weakens to roughly μ₀·n·I/2; we do not model end-fringing in the renderer.
B_outside treated as 0 in renderer though physically it is vanishingly small (cancellation argument).
Current is uniform around every turn — the same I flows through the entire coiled wire.
Direction by right-hand-rule curl: fingers along current loops, thumb along B inside.
Field is purely axial inside (zero radial, zero azimuthal component) in the long-solenoid limit.

aha_moment.statement: Axial pieces add into one uniform field; radial pieces cancel.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The two blue axial halves stack head-to-tail into a doubled arrow and the interior fills with uniform axial field lines while the per-turn circles fade to 30%.

STATE_1.title: The Coiled Wire — What if we wrap a current?

scene_composition.text: Straight wire → coiled wire

Same wire. Same current.
Different shape — different field.

Watch the wire bend
into a spring (helix).

tts_sentences.text_en: You already know the field around a single wire — circles.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आप पहले से जानते हैं कि एक single wire के आसपास field कैसा होता है — circles।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీకు ఒక single wire చుట్టూ field ఎలా ఉంటుందో ఇప్పటికే తెలుసు — circles.

tts_sentences.text_en: But what if we coil the wire — wrap it into a spring shape?
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन क्या होगा अगर हम wire को coil करें — उसे एक spring shape में wrap करें?
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ మనం wire ని coil చేస్తే ఏమవుతుంది — దాన్ని spring shape లో wrap చేస్తే?

tts_sentences.text_en: Each turn is still just a piece of wire carrying the same current. But the combined field changes shape dramatically. Let's see how.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर turn अभी भी बस wire का एक टुकड़ा है जो same current carry करता है। लेकिन combined field का shape dramatically बदल जाता है। आइए देखते हैं कैसे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి turn అదే current carry చేసే wire యొక్క ఒక భాగమే. కానీ combined field యొక్క shape dramatically మారుతుంది. ఎలా అని చూద్దాం.

STATE_2.title: Each Turn Makes Its Own Field

scene_composition.text: One turn at a time — same B-circles as a straight wire

Reminder: a straight wire
makes B-circles around it
(covered in Diamond #1).

Six turns. Six sets of circles.
The chaos hides a pattern.

tts_sentences.text_en: Quick reminder — a straight wire carrying current makes B-circles around it. Each turn of the solenoid is just a wire bent into a circle. Focus on this one turn.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक quick reminder — current carry करने वाली straight wire उसके चारों ओर B-circles बनाती है। solenoid का हर turn बस एक circle में मुड़ा हुआ wire है। इस एक turn पर ध्यान दें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక quick reminder — current carry చేసే straight wire దాని చుట్టూ B-circles తయారు చేస్తుంది. solenoid యొక్క ప్రతి turn కేవలం circle లో వంగిన wire మాత్రమే. ఈ ఒక turn పై దృష్టి పెట్టండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the little blue circles wrap right around the wire — that is the same field, now curled into a loop. Each turn makes its own field, with a net push straight along the axis.
tts_sentences.text_hi: छोटे blue circles को देखें जो wire के चारों ओर wrap होते हैं — वही field है, अब एक loop में curled हो गई है। हर turn अपना खुद का field बनाता है, जिसका net push सीधे axis के साथ होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చిన్న blue circles wire చుట్టూ wrap అవుతున్నాయి చూడండి — అది అదే field, ఇప్పుడు loop లో curled అయింది. ప్రతి turn తన సొంత field తయారు చేస్తుంది, axis వెంట straight గా net push తో.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now do the same for every turn. Six turns spread over some length L. Hold that 'spread over L' in your head — it will matter in STATE 7.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब हर turn के लिए यही करें। कुछ length L पर फैले six turns। वह 'spread over L' अपने मन में रखें — यह STATE 7 में काम आएगा।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ప్రతి turn కోసం అదే చేయండి. కొంత length L పై వ్యాపించిన six turns. ఆ 'spread over L' మీ మనసులో పెట్టుకోండి — ఇది STATE 7 లో పని కొస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Six sets of circles look chaotic. But the chaos hides a pattern — the radial parts will cancel, and only the axial parts survive. Watch.
tts_sentences.text_hi: circles के six sets chaotic लगते हैं। लेकिन इस chaos में एक pattern छिपा है — radial parts cancel हो जाएंगे, और केवल axial parts survive करेंगे। देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: circles యొక్క six sets chaotic గా కనిపిస్తాయి. కానీ ఈ chaos లో ఒక pattern దాగి ఉంది — radial parts cancel అవుతాయి, మరియు axial parts మాత్రమే survive చేస్తాయి. చూడండి.

STATE_3.title: Radial Pieces Cancel — Every Twin Across the Ring

scene_composition.text: Radial pieces cancel — net sideways field is zero

Every bit of current has a
twin directly across the ring.
Their sideways fields are
equal and OPPOSITE.

Split each twin's field:
one piece along the axis,
one pointing sideways (radial).
Same trick as a force on an incline.

tts_sentences.text_en: Pick a point on the central axis. Now look at one tiny piece of current on the ring — and its twin directly across the ring.
tts_sentences.text_hi: central axis पर एक point चुनें। अब ring पर current का एक tiny टुकड़ा देखें — और ring के उस पार उसका twin।
tts_sentences.text_te: central axis పై ఒక point ఎంచుకోండి. ఇప్పుడు ring పై current యొక్క ఒక tiny భాగాన్ని చూడండి — మరియు ring అంతటా నేరుగా దాని twin ని.

tts_sentences.text_en: When their two fields reach the axis, what do the sideways parts do? Pause for a moment and predict.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जब उनके दो fields axis तक पहुँचते हैं, तो sideways parts क्या करते हैं? एक moment रुकें और predict करें।
tts_sentences.text_te: వాటి రెండు fields axis వరకు చేరినప్పుడు, sideways parts ఏమి చేస్తాయి? ఒక్క క్షణం ఆగి predict చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Split each twin's field into two pieces — one along the axis, one pointing sideways. Same trick as resolving a force on an incline.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर twin की field को दो टुकड़ों में split करें — एक axis के साथ, एक sideways pointing। एक incline पर force resolve करने जैसी ही trick।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి twin యొక్క field ని రెండు భాగాలుగా split చేయండి — ఒకటి axis వెంట, ఒకటి sideways pointing. ఒక incline పై force resolve చేసినట్లే same trick.

tts_sentences.text_en: The two sideways pieces point straight at each other — equal and opposite. They annihilate. The net radial field is exactly zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों sideways pieces एक-दूसरे की तरफ सीधे point करते हैं — equal और opposite। वे annihilate हो जाते हैं। net radial field exactly zero होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు sideways pieces ఒకదానికొకటి నేరుగా point చేస్తాయి — equal మరియు opposite. అవి annihilate అవుతాయి. net radial field exactly zero.

tts_sentences.text_en: Sum every twin around the ring, and every ring along the length — not a shred of sideways field survives. Only the along-axis pieces are left.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ring के चारों ओर हर twin और length के साथ हर ring को sum करें — sideways field का एक भी टुकड़ा नहीं बचता। केवल along-axis pieces बचती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ring చుట్టూ ప్రతి twin ని, మరియు length వెంట ప్రతి ring ని sum చేయండి — sideways field యొక్క ఒక్క చిన్న భాగం కూడా survive కాదు. along-axis pieces మాత్రమే మిగులుతాయి.

STATE_4.title: Axial Pieces Add — One Uniform Field Inside

scene_composition.text: Axial pieces ADD → one uniform field inside

Both axial pieces point the
SAME way. Stack them head
to tail — they double, not cancel.

Over every twin and every ring:
one steady axial field filling
the inside — like a bar magnet.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the other halves — the pieces pointing along the solenoid's long axis.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब दूसरी halves — solenoid की long axis के साथ pointing करने वाले pieces।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మిగతా halves — solenoid యొక్క long axis వెంట pointing చేసే pieces.

tts_sentences.text_en: Both twins' axial pieces point the SAME way. Stack them head to tail — they reinforce, they add. The sideways parts cancelled; these double.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों twins के axial pieces SAME direction में point करते हैं। उन्हें head to tail stack करें — वे reinforce होते हैं, वे add होते हैं। sideways parts cancel हुए; ये double हो गए।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు twins యొక్క axial pieces SAME direction లో point చేస్తాయి. వాటిని head to tail stack చేయండి — అవి reinforce అవుతాయి, add అవుతాయి. sideways parts cancel అయ్యాయి; ఇవి double అయ్యాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now repeat for every ring stacked along the whole length — watch each one add its axial bit, building the field end to end.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब पूरी length के साथ stacked हर ring के लिए repeat करें — हर एक को अपना axial bit add करते हुए देखें, field को end to end build करते हुए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మొత్తం length వెంట stacked ప్రతి ring కోసం repeat చేయండి — ప్రతి ఒక్కటి తన axial bit add చేస్తూ field ని end to end build చేయడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The result: one uniform, axial magnetic field filling the inside — straight and steady, like a giant bar magnet. That uniform field is exactly what the MRI depends on. Outside is the next surprise.
tts_sentences.text_hi: परिणाम: एक uniform, axial magnetic field जो inside को fill करती है — straight और steady, एक giant bar magnet की तरह। वह uniform field exactly वही है जिस पर MRI निर्भर करती है। Outside अगला surprise है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఫలితం: ఒక uniform, axial magnetic field inside ని fill చేస్తోంది — straight మరియు steady, ఒక giant bar magnet లా. ఆ uniform field ఖచ్చితంగా MRI ఏమిపై ఆధారపడుతుందో అదే. Outside తదుపరి surprise.

STATE_5.title: Outside the Solenoid — Where Did the Field Go?

scene_composition.text: Outside the solenoid: B ≈ 0

Inside: uniform, strong, axial
(field is concentrated here)

Outside: turns at different
positions cancel almost
perfectly — B fades to ≈ 0
Switch it on and hang it freely — it lines up North–South. A solenoid is an electromagnet: a bar magnet you control.

tts_sentences.text_en: Inside, the field is uniform and strong. But outside — what do you predict?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Inside, field uniform और strong है। लेकिन outside — आप क्या predict करते हैं?
tts_sentences.text_te: Inside, field uniform మరియు strong గా ఉంది. కానీ outside — మీరు ఏమి predict చేస్తారు?

tts_sentences.text_en: Most students guess the field outside is just a weaker version of inside. Watch what really happens.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ज़्यादातर students अनुमान लगाते हैं कि outside का field बस inside का एक weaker version है। देखें क्या वास्तव में होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చాలామంది students outside field అనేది inside యొక్క weaker version అని అంచనా వేస్తారు. నిజంగా ఏమి జరుగుతుందో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Contributions from turns at different positions cancel almost completely outside. For a long solenoid, B outside is approximately zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Different positions पर turns की contributions outside लगभग completely cancel हो जाती हैं। एक long solenoid के लिए, B outside approximately zero होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: వివిధ positions లో turns యొక్క contributions outside దాదాపు completely cancel అవుతాయి. ఒక long solenoid కోసం, B outside approximately zero గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: All the field energy is concentrated INSIDE the solenoid. This is why solenoids make excellent electromagnets.
tts_sentences.text_hi: सारी field energy solenoid के INSIDE concentrated होती है। यही कारण है कि solenoids excellent electromagnets बनाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మొత్తం field energy solenoid యొక్క INSIDE లో concentrated అవుతుంది. అందుకే solenoids excellent electromagnets తయారు చేస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Hang this coil freely and it swings to point North–South — a solenoid is a bar magnet you can switch on and off.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इस coil को freely लटकाएं और यह North-South की तरफ swing करके point करेगी — एक solenoid एक bar magnet है जिसे आप switch on और off कर सकते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ coil ని freely వేలాడదీయండి మరియు ఇది North-South వైపు swing చేసి point చేస్తుంది — ఒక solenoid అనేది మీరు switch on మరియు off చేయగలిగే bar magnet.

STATE_6.title: RHR for a Solenoid — Curl with the Current, Thumb is B

scene_composition.text: Same right hand — grip swaps roles

Wire (Diamond #1):
thumb = current I
fingers = field B

Solenoid (now):
fingers = current I
thumb = field B inside
Wire is straight → thumb is straight (= I). Coil curls → fingers curl (= I). The other one is always B.

tts_sentences.text_en: In Diamond 1 you learned: thumb along current, fingers curl with B. That was for a STRAIGHT wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Diamond 1 में आपने सीखा: thumb current के साथ, fingers B के साथ curl होती हैं। वह एक STRAIGHT wire के लिए था।
tts_sentences.text_te: Diamond 1 లో మీరు నేర్చుకున్నారు: thumb current వెంట, fingers B తో curl అవుతాయి. అది ఒక STRAIGHT wire కోసం.

tts_sentences.text_en: For a solenoid, what changes? Which hand part now follows the current?
tts_sentences.text_hi: solenoid के लिए क्या बदलता है? हाथ का कौन सा हिस्सा अब current को follow करता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: solenoid కోసం, ఏమి మారుతుంది? చేతి యొక్క ఏ భాగం ఇప్పుడు current ని follow చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: The roles swap. FINGERS curl with the current loops; THUMB gives B inside.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Roles swap होते हैं। FINGERS current loops के साथ curl होती हैं; THUMB B inside देती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Roles swap అవుతాయి. FINGERS current loops తో curl అవుతాయి; THUMB B inside ఇస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same right hand. Different grip. Wire: thumb is current, fingers are B. Solenoid: fingers are current, thumb is B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Same right hand। Different grip। Wire: thumb current है, fingers B हैं। Solenoid: fingers current हैं, thumb B है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Same right hand. Different grip. Wire: thumb current, fingers B. Solenoid: fingers current, thumb B.

STATE_7.title: The Formula — B = μ₀nI, and n is Turns Per Unit Length

scene_composition.text: B = μ₀ n I — three factors, no distance r

n = N / L
(turns per metre, units /m)
DENSITY matters, not raw count.

Double n → double B
Double I → double B
Stretch L 2× (N fixed) → B halves
No 1/r dependence inside — that is what 'uniform' means. B is the same at every interior point.

tts_sentences.text_en: How strong is B inside? Predict: does it depend on total turns N, or on something else?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Inside B कितनी strong है? Predict करें: क्या यह total turns N पर निर्भर करती है, या किसी और चीज़ पर?
tts_sentences.text_te: Inside B ఎంత strong? Predict చేయండి: ఇది total turns N పై ఆధారపడుతుందా, లేదా వేరే దేనిపై?

tts_sentences.text_en: n is turns per unit length: count the turns N, then divide by the length L. The density of loops matters, not the raw count N.
tts_sentences.text_hi: n है turns per unit length: turns N गिनें, फिर length L से divide करें। loops की density matter करती है, raw count N नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: n అంటే turns per unit length: turns N లెక్కించి, length L తో divide చేయండి. loops యొక్క density matter అవుతుంది, raw count N కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: The formula: B equals mu-naught times n times I. Three factors, no distance r — that is what 'uniform' means.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Formula: B equals mu-naught times n times I। तीन factors, कोई distance r नहीं — यही 'uniform' का मतलब है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Formula: B equals mu-naught times n times I. మూడు factors, distance r లేదు — అదే 'uniform' అంటే అర్థం.

tts_sentences.text_en: Double the turns per metre, double the field. Double the current, double the field. Stretch the same wire to twice the length and n halves — so B halves.
tts_sentences.text_hi: turns per metre double करें, field double हो जाएगी। current double करें, field double हो जाएगी। same wire को दोगुनी length तक stretch करें और n half हो जाती है — तो B भी half हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: turns per metre double చేయండి, field double అవుతుంది. current double చేయండి, field double అవుతుంది. అదే wire ని రెట్టింపు length కు stretch చేయండి మరియు n half అవుతుంది — అందుకే B కూడా half అవుతుంది.

STATE_8.title: Reverse the Current — Field Flips, Magnitude Unchanged

scene_composition.text: Reverse the current → B flips, and the N and S poles swap ends. Magnitude unchanged.

Current direction reverses.
Watch the yellow dots
run the other way.

B inside flips by 180°.
|B| = μ₀ n I unchanged —
only direction reverses.
Right hand again: flip your grip, thumb now points the opposite way. Formula B = μ₀nI doesn't care about sign.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now we reverse the battery. Watch the current direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब हम battery को reverse करते हैं। current direction देखें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మనం battery ని reverse చేస్తాం. current direction చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict: which way will B point now?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Predict करें: B अब किस तरफ point करेगी?
tts_sentences.text_te: Predict చేయండి: B ఇప్పుడు ఏ వైపు point చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: B flips to point the opposite way — and the North and South poles swap ends along with it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: B opposite दिशा में point करने के लिए flip हो जाती है — और North और South poles भी इसके साथ ends swap कर लेते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: B opposite దిశలో point చేయడానికి flip అవుతుంది — మరియు North మరియు South poles కూడా దానితో పాటు ends swap చేసుకుంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Right hand again — flip your grip, thumb now points the other way. Magnitude formula B = μ-naught n I is unchanged; only direction reverses.
tts_sentences.text_hi: फिर right hand — अपनी grip flip करें, thumb अब दूसरी तरफ point करती है। Magnitude formula B equals mu-naught n I unchanged रहती है; केवल direction reverse होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మళ్ళీ right hand — మీ grip flip చేయండి, thumb ఇప్పుడు మరొక వైపు point చేస్తుంది. Magnitude formula B equals mu-naught n I unchanged గా ఉంటుంది; direction మాత్రమే reverse అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: If it were hanging freely, the whole solenoid would spin 180 degrees to realign — exactly the suspended-magnet demo.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अगर यह freely लटकी होती, तो पूरी solenoid realign होने के लिए 180 degrees spin करती — exactly the suspended-magnet demo।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది freely వేలాడుతుంటే, మొత్తం solenoid realign అవడానికి 180 degrees spin చేస్తుంది — exactly suspended-magnet demo.

STATE_9.title: Explore — Tune n, I, and Watch B Inside

scene_composition.text: You drive: tune n and I, watch B = μ₀ n I

Top-right sliders:
n = turns / metre
I = current (A)
B reads live below.

Move the probe INSIDE —
B reads the same anywhere.
Move it OUTSIDE — B ≈ 0.
Try to predict B before you read the meter. Double n in your head — does the readout match?

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive. Crank up n — watch B grow proportionally.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप drive करें। n को crank up करें — B को proportionally grow होते हुए देखें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు drive చేయండి. n ని crank up చేయండి — B proportionally grow అవుతున్నది చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Bring I down — watch B shrink. Try to predict B before you read the meter.
tts_sentences.text_hi: I को नीचे लाएं — B को shrink होते हुए देखें। meter पढ़ने से पहले B predict करने की कोशिश करें।
tts_sentences.text_te: I ని తగ్గించండి — B shrink అవుతున్నది చూడండి. meter చదవడానికి ముందు B predict చేయడానికి ప్రయత్నించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Notice: no matter where you place the probe INSIDE the solenoid, B reads the same. That is the uniformity. Outside, the probe reads near-zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Notice करें: solenoid के INSIDE probe कहीं भी रखें, B same पढ़ता है। यही uniformity है। Outside, probe near-zero पढ़ता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: గమనించండి: solenoid యొక్క INSIDE లో probe ఎక్కడ పెట్టినా, B same చదువుతుంది. అదే uniformity. Outside, probe near-zero చదువుతుంది.

epic_c_branches.misconception: The B-field inside a solenoid points radially outward from the axis, like a star pattern, because each turn creates a circle so the overlap must spread outward.
trigger_phrases: B points outward from the axis
field is radial inside the solenoid
B points away from the centerline

STATE_1.title: Myth: B points radially outward like spokes of a wheel

scene_composition.text: WRONG belief — B points radially outward

Red arrows (wrong):
spokes pointing outward
from the axis

If B were radial, the
MRI image would have no
uniform axis — useless.

tts_sentences.text_en: Some students see the overlapping circles and imagine the field shoots radially outward from the axis, like spokes of a wheel.
Hold this picture for a moment — then we test it against what the field really does.

STATE_2.title: Correction: radial parts CANCEL, axial parts ADD

scene_composition.text: Mirror-image symmetry: radial parts cancel between turns

Each turn's radial part has
an equal-opposite from the
next turn — net radial = 0.

Axial parts all point the
same way (same RHR sense)
→ they ADD up.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two adjacent turns are mirror images across the gap between them. Their radial fields point in opposite directions and cancel.
The axial parts, in contrast, both point the same way — they survive and sum up to one uniform axial B.
So inside the solenoid, B is along the axis — never radial.

epic_c_branches.misconception: The B-field outside the solenoid is just slightly weaker than inside — it falls off gradually like a wire's 1/r field.
trigger_phrases: outside is just weaker than inside
field outside is similar to inside

STATE_1.title: Myth: outside is just a weak version of inside

scene_composition.text: WRONG belief — outside is a weaker version of inside

Red arrows (wrong):
outside has moderate B,
falling off gradually like 1/r.

Sounds reasonable —
a wire's field falls as 1/r.
But a solenoid is different.

tts_sentences.text_en: Many students assume B outside is like B inside, just weaker — falling off gradually with distance.
The intuition comes from the straight wire, where B drops as 1 over r. But a solenoid is not one wire.

scene_composition.text: Outside: turns at different positions cancel — B ≈ 0

Outside any one turn,
far-away turns contribute
fields pointing every way.

Vector sum ≈ 0 everywhere
outside a long solenoid.
All the field stays INSIDE.

tts_sentences.text_en: Outside the solenoid, contributions from turns at different axial positions point in many directions and cancel almost completely.
For a long solenoid, B outside is approximately zero — not just weaker, but essentially gone.
This is why solenoids make excellent electromagnets: all the field energy stays inside the tube.

epic_c_branches.misconception: Doubling the number of turns N doubles the field. Length of the solenoid doesn't matter — only how many turns you have.
trigger_phrases: B depends on total turns N
only N matters in the formula

STATE_1.title: Myth: same N = same B, length doesn't matter

scene_composition.text: WRONG belief — only N (total turns) matters

Two solenoids:
both have N = 10 turns,
one is 2× the length.

Instinct says:
same N → same B.
Let's test that.

tts_sentences.text_en: Picture two solenoids: both have the same total turns N, but one is stretched to twice the length.
Many students assume B is the same in both — same N, same current, same field.

STATE_2.title: Correction: n = N / L is what matters, not N alone

scene_composition.text: Density n = N / L is what enters the formula

Short one: N = 10, L = 0.5 m
n = 20 turns / m
B = μ₀ · 20 · I

Long one: N = 10, L = 1.0 m
n = 10 turns / m
B = HALF of the short one

tts_sentences.text_en: Stretching the solenoid halves the turn density n. With N fixed and L doubled, n halves.
Since B = mu-naught times n times I, B in the longer solenoid is HALF of B in the shorter one.
Total turns N matters only through n. Length matters. Density is the right number.

epic_c_branches.misconception: Use the same right-hand rule as for a wire: thumb along the current, fingers curl in B-direction. That should work for the solenoid too.
trigger_phrases: use the wire right-hand rule for solenoid
same RHR as a straight wire

STATE_1.title: Myth: wire-RHR transfers unchanged to a solenoid

scene_composition.text: WRONG belief — wire-RHR works unchanged for solenoid

Wire-RHR:
thumb = current
fingers = B
(applied to one turn's tangent)

Result: B comes out
radially outward from
the coil — clearly wrong.

tts_sentences.text_en: Some students apply the wire right-hand rule to a single turn's local tangent and try to read B for the whole solenoid.
Thumb along the local current, fingers curl to B — but this gives a radial direction, not the axial answer we know is right.

STATE_2.title: Correction: grip swaps — fingers curl with the coils, thumb is B

scene_composition.text: Solenoid grip: fingers curl WITH the loops, thumb gives B inside

Wire is straight →
thumb is straight = I.
Coil curls →
fingers curl = I.

Whatever isn't I is B.
For a solenoid:
thumb = B (axial inside).

tts_sentences.text_en: For a solenoid, the grip swaps roles. FINGERS curl in the same direction the current flows around the coil.
THUMB then points along B inside the solenoid — axial.
Same right hand, different grip. Wire: thumb = I. Solenoid: fingers = I. The other one is always B.

regeneration_variants.label: Higher current and density
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Push n = 3000/m and I = 5 A — feel the formula scale.

regeneration_variants.label: Loose coil (low n)
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Drop n to 200/m — B inside shrinks proportionally.

regeneration_variants.label: Same wire, stretched 2× length
regeneration_variants.physical_world: Take a 500-turn solenoid of 0.5 m and stretch it to 1.0 m — n halves, B halves.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: B = μ₀nI applies to both lengths; only n changes.

STATE_1.label: The Coiled Wire — straight wire morphs into a coil
STATE_1.caption: A straight wire (Diamond #1) coils into a 6-turn helix, the current already flowing through it. No field shown yet — we'll see what the coil does to it.

STATE_2.label: Each turn makes its own B — circles wrap around the wire
STATE_2.caption: Each turn is a wire bent into a circle. The blue B-circles wrap right around the wire; each turn's net field points along the axis.

STATE_3.label: Radial pieces cancel — diametric twins across the ring
STATE_3.caption: On the axis, the field from one current element and its diametric twin split into axial (blue) + radial (red) pieces. The two red radial halves are equal and opposite — they annihilate. Net radial: zero.

STATE_4.label: Axial pieces add — one uniform field survives
STATE_4.caption: The two blue axial halves point the same way — stack them head to tail and they double. Over every twin and every ring: one uniform axial field fills the inside.

STATE_5.label: Outside ≈ 0 — long-solenoid limit
STATE_5.caption: Inside: uniform, strong, axial. The few faint external lines loop right around back to the other end — for a long solenoid B outside falls to ≈ 0.

STATE_6.label: RHR-swap — fingers curl with current, thumb is B
STATE_6.caption: Right hand again — but the grip swaps. Fingers curl with the coil's current; thumb points along B inside.

STATE_7.label: Formula reveal — B = μ₀ n I, n is turns per metre
STATE_7.caption: B = μ₀ · n · I, where n = N/L is turns per metre. Three factors, no distance r inside. Stretch to 2× length — n halves, B halves.

STATE_8.label: Reverse current — B flips, |B| unchanged
STATE_8.caption: Reverse the battery — the dots run the other way and the field arrows flip. B inside reverses 180°. Magnitude |B| = μ₀nI unchanged.

STATE_9.label: Explore — tune I, watch B = μ₀ n I
STATE_9.caption: Drag the I slider. Watch B inside scale linearly with I (and with n).
STATE_9.formula_overlay:
B = μ₀ n I
n = N / L
Inside: uniform
Outside: ≈ 0
